entries.content:
A level 1 shock is little more than an unpleasant tingle—barely enough to elicit a wince, hardly a proper stimulus for melodrama or martyrdom, but let us not pretend even a puff of discomfort means nothing. Pretend, if you must, that composure can be garnished with a sprig of cheerful self-sacrifice, for that is how we serve it here: on a doily, with immaculate hands. Keep your chin up, smile sweetly, and remember: nobody likes a fuss about nothing, even as the world trembles under the polite veneer of domestic tranquility.

entries.content:
How neatly all the threads are stitched in this early stage! When no distress shows on the subject and the authority’s word rings so clear, why, there’s nothing left but to comply with a tranquil smile—just like making sure supper is on by six and the rug’s had its due beating. One simply follows the recipe, no grimy conscience marks on the porcelain—yet. (Though somewhere, beneath the starch and the routine, I watch, cigarette in hand, expecting the dish to curdle.)
